methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

data.narrative:
Skyroot Aerospace is a Series C-stage space launch company that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 same-stage private peers in Space & Aerospace (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace) and 5 public sector benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $95M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yields $162M. Peer-median anchoring across the five same-sector comparables produces $1.5B, with the peer set spanning $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp P/S anchoring at a 45.3x median multiple cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures.

The two-method triangulation supports a range of $137M to $1.7B, with a median of $831M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: stage-progression relies on elevated assumptions about capital efficiency, while peer-median assumes market-standard risk-adjusted pricing for this cohort. Key uncertainties include product development risk, path to revenue, and whether peers command premiums tied to specific technical or market milestones not uniformly distributed across the set. The $831M midpoint balances these methods.
